title: Dates and Times
description: Work with DateTime objects and functions.

slides.title: The DateTime Class
slides.content:
# Modern Date/Time Handling

PHP's `DateTime` class provides an object-oriented way to work with dates and times.

```php
$now = new DateTime(); // current date/time
echo $now->format('Y-m-d H:i:s');

$birthday = new DateTime('1990-05-15');
```

You can also use `date_create()` procedural style, but OOP is recommended.

slides.title: Formatting and Modifying
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$date = new DateTime('2025-03-17');
echo $date->format('l, F j, Y') . "\n";
$date->modify('+1 month');
echo $date->format('Y-m-d') . "\n";
?>

slides.title: Date Differences
Date Comparison
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$date1 = new DateTime('2025-03-17');
$date2 = new DateTime('2025-03-18');
if ($date1 < $date2) {
    echo '$date1 is earlier';
}
?>

quizData.question: Which method returns the difference between two DateTime objects?
quizData.explanation: diff() returns a DateInterval object representing the difference.
